meta.title: Protestant vs. Orthodox Dialogue: Gavin Ortlund and Craig Truglia
meta.author: Gavin Ortlund

questions.question: What Latin phrase does Ortlund use to describe Protestantism's principle of being 'always reforming'?
options: Sola fide
Semper reformanda
Ad fontes
Ex cathedra
questions.answer: Semper reformanda

questions.question: What was the decisive scriptural passage that led Truglia toward Orthodoxy?
options: Romans 6:1-4
1 Corinthians 12:3
Galatians 5:19-21
Matthew 12:33
questions.answer: Galatians 5:19-21

questions.question: Which Greek word in Galatians 5 does Truglia translate as 'dissensions'?
options: Haireseis
Schismata
Dichostasiai
Koinonia
questions.answer: Dichostasiai

questions.question: Which church father does Truglia quote at length on schism being worse than any reformation could compensate?
options: Cyprian of Carthage
Irenaeus of Lyon
Ignatius of Antioch
Augustine of Hippo
questions.answer: Irenaeus of Lyon

questions.question: According to Ortlund, what is the Protestant claim about the church's visibility?
options: The church is entirely invisible
The church is visible but not restricted to one institution
The church has no visible form at all
Only local congregations are the true church
questions.answer: The church is visible but not restricted to one institution

questions.question: What does Ortlund argue Satan cannot produce, even if he can do fake miracles?
options: Theological knowledge
Church institutions
Genuine spiritual fruit like joy and charity
Liturgical worship
questions.answer: Genuine spiritual fruit like joy and charity

questions.question: When Ortlund directly asks Truglia whether he thinks Ortlund is damned, what is Truglia's answer?
options: He evades the question
He says it is unknowable
He affirms the Cyprianic hardline: yes
He says only God can judge
questions.answer: He affirms the Cyprianic hardline: yes

questions.question: What does Truglia mean by the 'material sufficiency' of Scripture?
options: Scripture contains the physical materials for worship
Every doctrine necessary for salvation is contained in the Bible
Scripture is sufficient without any interpretive tradition
Only the material portions of Scripture are authoritative
questions.answer: Every doctrine necessary for salvation is contained in the Bible

questions.question: According to Truglia, why is he Orthodox rather than Roman Catholic despite both claiming tradition?
options: Rome has better liturgy but worse theology
Rome sincerely holds to Vincent of Lérins's Canon
Rome believes in doctrinal development and rejects Vincent's Canon, making their appeal to tradition insincere
Rome accepts the filioque while Orthodoxy does not
questions.answer: Rome believes in doctrinal development and rejects Vincent's Canon, making their appeal to tradition insincere

questions.question: What is the Vincent of Lérins Canon?
options: The list of books in the biblical canon established by Vincent
The principle that true doctrine is what has been believed everywhere, always, and by all
A 5th-century rule for monastic liturgical practice
The canon law governing papal elections
questions.answer: The principle that true doctrine is what has been believed everywhere, always, and by all

questions.question: Which passage does Ortlund cite to argue that factions in the church do not necessarily mean one group is the true church and the others are out?
options: Galatians 5:19-21
1 Corinthians 1:10-13
Romans 16:17
Titus 1:9
questions.answer: 1 Corinthians 1:10-13

questions.question: What New Testament event does Ortlund cite as a split that did not result in one party being outside the church?
options: The Council of Jerusalem's decree
Paul's sharp disagreement with Barnabas in Acts 15
Peter's denial of Christ
The stoning of Stephen
questions.answer: Paul's sharp disagreement with Barnabas in Acts 15

questions.question: In Ortlund's argument, what kind of groups was Cyprian addressing when he said 'outside the church there is no salvation'?
options: Anglicans and Lutherans
Marcionites and Gnostics
Eastern Orthodox Christians
Donatists and Arians
questions.answer: Marcionites and Gnostics

questions.question: How many total Christians does Ortlund estimate existed when Cyprian made his hardline statements?
options: Ten million
Five hundred thousand
One million
Ten thousand
questions.answer: One million

questions.question: What archaeological evidence does Truglia cite for early Christian icon veneration in Jerusalem?
options: A mosaic in a basilica with an inscription about worship
The Grotto with an inscription stating 'under this image we venerate'
A catacomb painting with liturgical instructions
A carved stone altar with images of saints
questions.answer: The Grotto with an inscription stating 'under this image we venerate'

questions.question: What does Truglia argue about Tertullian's complaint regarding an image on the chalice?
options: It proves Christians rejected all images
It shows Christians were already placing Christ's image on sacred objects, attesting to early iconodulia
It demonstrates that chalices had no religious significance
It confirms Tertullian supported icon veneration
questions.answer: It shows Christians were already placing Christ's image on sacred objects, attesting to early iconodulia

questions.question: How does Truglia handle the usual anti-icon citation of Epiphanius?
options: He agrees Epiphanius proves aniconic worship
He dismisses Epiphanius as a heretic
He notes Epiphanius admitted no one listened to him and recorded the majority's defense of veneration
He argues Epiphanius was actually pro-icon
questions.answer: He notes Epiphanius admitted no one listened to him and recorded the majority's defense of veneration

questions.question: What analogy does Truglia use to reinterpret Origen's statement that Christians have 'no altars and images'?
options: Origen was speaking only about house churches
If 'no altars' means no pagan altars (since Christian altars existed), then 'no images' means no pagan images
Origen was describing an ideal rather than actual practice
Origen was writing before any Christian art existed
questions.answer: If 'no altars' means no pagan altars (since Christian altars existed), then 'no images' means no pagan images

questions.question: Who does Truglia identify as the only figure before the 5th century to break with institutional ecclesiology?
options: Augustine of Hippo
Origen of Alexandria
Tyconius of Carthage
Eusebius of Caesarea
questions.answer: Tyconius of Carthage

questions.question: Why does Truglia consider Tyconius a weak precedent for non-institutional ecclesiology?
options: Tyconius wrote too late to be relevant
Tyconius was himself a schismatic, not even in communion with the Donatists
Tyconius's works have been lost
Tyconius was a Roman Catholic, not a Protestant
questions.answer: Tyconius was himself a schismatic, not even in communion with the Donatists

questions.question: What does Ortlund say about the mainstream Protestant view of the church between the 1st and 16th centuries?
options: The true church vanished and was restored at the Reformation
The true church persisted for every nanosecond; errors accumulated but the church never disappeared
The true church moved to the East during the medieval period
The church was invisible until Luther rediscovered it
questions.answer: The true church persisted for every nanosecond; errors accumulated but the church never disappeared

questions.question: What comparison does Ortlund draw to challenge the Orthodox claim that Protestants are outside the church?
options: He compares it to the Roman Catholic claim about Orthodox Christians
He compares it to the True Orthodox who make the same argument against mainstream Orthodoxy
He compares it to the Jehovah's Witness view of Christendom
He compares it to the Donatist controversy
questions.answer: He compares it to the True Orthodox who make the same argument against mainstream Orthodoxy

questions.question: What two doctrinal issues does Ortlund name as examples where he believes the Orthodox claim to unchanged apostolic doctrine fails?
options: Baptism and the Eucharist
The filioque and the veneration of icons
Predestination and free will
Papal infallibility and purgatory
questions.answer: The filioque and the veneration of icons

questions.question: What 'moral hazard' argument does Truglia make regarding the interpretation of schism?
options: Schism is always morally justified if done for doctrinal reasons
Even if there is a 50/50 interpretive standoff, one should not gamble on the position every church father rejected
The moral hazard is in staying in a tradition that might be wrong
There is no moral dimension to ecclesiological questions
questions.answer: Even if there is a 50/50 interpretive standoff, one should not gamble on the position every church father rejected

questions.question: According to Ortlund, what does he cite from Augustine's letters to counter the claim of a patristic consensus on hardline ecclesiology?
options: Augustine denied the existence of heretical groups
Augustine said there can be true Christians and people of good sincerity among heretical groups
Augustine agreed entirely with Cyprian on all points
Augustine rejected the concept of the visible church
questions.answer: Augustine said there can be true Christians and people of good sincerity among heretical groups

questions.question: What concern does Ortlund express about Orthodox responses to his interview with Josh Shuping?
options: The responses were theologically sophisticated but wrong
Many comments were shockingly hateful, personally demeaning, and went beyond charitable argumentation
The responses ignored his arguments entirely
Orthodox viewers refused to watch the video
questions.answer: Many comments were shockingly hateful, personally demeaning, and went beyond charitable argumentation

questions.question: What does Ortlund say about Satan's role in inter-traditional exchanges?
options: Satan controls one side of the debate
Satan gains when Christians hate each other; charitable communication commends the gospel
Satan is irrelevant to theological disagreements
Satan only works through heretical groups
questions.answer: Satan gains when Christians hate each other; charitable communication commends the gospel

questions.question: What observation does Truglia make about why people in apologetics sometimes become angry and hateful?
options: They are motivated by pure theological conviction
Anger often betrays insecurity and lack of confidence in one's own position
They are following the example of the church fathers
Anger is a sign of the Holy Spirit's conviction
questions.answer: Anger often betrays insecurity and lack of confidence in one's own position

questions.question: Which book of Irenaeus does Truglia cite for the argument that apostolic tradition is preserved through the succession of presbyters in the churches?
options: Against Heresies Book I
Against Heresies Book II
Against Heresies Book III
Against Heresies Book V
questions.answer: Against Heresies Book III

questions.question: What quote does Truglia use to close the show?
options: 'The truth shall set you free' — Jesus Christ
'Fight to the death for the truth, and the Lord God will fight for you' — Jesus ben Sirach
'In essentials unity, in non-essentials liberty' — Augustine
'Here I stand, I can do no other' — Martin Luther
questions.answer: 'Fight to the death for the truth, and the Lord God will fight for you' — Jesus ben Sirach
